This sentence is grammatically correct because it uses the word "buffalo" in three different ways: as a proper noun (the city in New York), as a verb (meaning to intimidate or confuse), and as a plural noun (the animal). The sentence can be parsed as "Buffalo buffalo (that) Buffalo buffalo buffalo, buffalo Buffalo buffalo," meaning bison from Buffalo that are intimidated by other bison from Buffalo also intimidate bison from Buffalo. Despite its confusing appearance, it follows standard English grammar rules.
